zh.Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial Amer: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州哈利法可
zh.lifeSummary: 當William R Magby於1790年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州哈利法可時，他的父親Vardry Magby II31歲，母親Rachel Atkinson31歲。他於1819年5月30日與Elizabeth Dunn在美國阿拉巴馬州聖 克萊爾結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1820年住在美國阿拉巴馬州聖 克萊爾且在美國阿拉巴馬州沃克住了大約10年。他去世於1869年7月31日在美國阿拉巴馬州麥迪遜享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William R Magby于1790年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州哈利法可时，他的父亲Vardry Magby II31岁，母亲Rachel Atkinson31岁。他于1819年5月30日与Elizabeth Dunn在美国亚拉巴马州聖 克萊爾结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1820年住在美国亚拉巴马州聖 克萊爾且在美国亚拉巴马州沃克住了大约10年。他去世于1869年7月31日在美国亚拉巴马州麥迪遜享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Magby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: McBee
meanings: Scottish: variant of McBay , itself a shortened form of McBeth . In Gaelic, -th- is silent.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Bill of Rights guarantees individual freedom.
Historical Boundaries: 1808: Madison, Mississippi Territory, United States 1817: Madison, Alabama Territory, United States 1819: Madison, Alabama, United States
War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.
Historical Boundaries: 1818: St. Clair, Alabama Territory, United States 1819: St. Clair, Alabama, United States

historicEvents.title: The Black Hawk War
historicEvents.description: Convinced that a group of Native American tribes were hostile, The United States formed a frontier militia to stop them in their tracks. Even though Black Hawk was hoping to avoid bloodshed while trying to resettle on tribal land, U.S. officials opened fire on the Native Americans. Black Hawk then responded to this confrontation by successfully attacking the militia at the Battle of Stillman's Run and then left northward. After a few months the militia caught up with Black Hawk and his men and defeated them at the Battle of Wisconsin Heights. While being weakened by hunger, injuries and desertion, Black Hawk and the rest of the many native survivors retreated towards the Mississippi. Unfortunately, Black Hawk and other leaders were later captured when they surrendered to the US forces and were then imprisoned for a year.

historicEvents.title: Angry Mob Seizes U-S Mail
historicEvents.description: On August 31, 1835, in Charleston, South Carolina an angry mob takes control over the U-S mail and burns it in public.
In 1860, South Carolina quit the United States because its citizens were in favor of slavery and President Lincoln was not. The Civil War started a year later.
Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

en.lifeSummary: When William R Magby was born in 1790, in Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial America, his father, Vardry Magby II, was 31 and his mother, Rachel Atkinson, was 31. He married Elizabeth Dunn on 30 May 1819, in St. Clair, Alabama, United States. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. He lived in St. Clair, Alabama, United States in 1820 and Walker, Alabama, United States for about 10 years. He died on 31 July 1869, in Madison, Alabama, United States, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William R Magby est né en 1790, à Halifax, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, Vardry Magby II, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Rachel Atkinson, avait 31 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Dunn le 30 mai 1819, à St. Clair, Alabama, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Il a vécu à St. Clair, Alabama, États-Unis en 1820 et à Walker, Alabama, États-Unis pendant environ 10 ans. Il est décédé le 31 juillet 1869, à Madison, Alabama, États-Unis, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William R Magby 1790 in Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial America geboren wurde, war sein Vater Vardry Magby II 31 und seine Mutter Rachel Atkinson 31. Er hat am 30. Mai 1819 in St. Clair, Alabama, Vereinigte Staaten Elizabeth Dunn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. 1820 hat er in St. Clair, Alabama, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt, und ungefähr 10 Jahre lang hat er in Walker, Alabama, Vereinigte Staaten gelebt. Er starb am 31. Juli 1869 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Madison, Alabama, Vereinigte Staaten.

Lui sposato Elizabeth Dunn il 30 maggio 1819, nel St. Clair, Alabama, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 31 luglio 1869, nel Madison, Alabama, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William R Magbyが1790年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ハリファックスで生まれた時，父Vardry Magby IIは31歳，母Rachel Atkinsonは31歳であった。1819年5月30日にアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州スト. クレイルでElizabeth Dunnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1820年にはアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州スト. クレイルに，アメリカ合衆国アラバマ州ウォーカーに約10年間住んでいた。彼は1869年7月31日にアメリカ合衆国アラバマ州マディソンで79歳で死去した。

ko.Halifax, Virginia, British Colonial Amer: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 할이파스
ko.lifeSummary: William R Magby(이)가 1790년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 할이파스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Vardry Magby II(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Rachel Atkinson(은)는 31세였다. 그는 1819년 5월 30일 미국 앨라배마 주 스트. 클레이르에서 Elizabeth Dunn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1820년도에 미국 앨라배마 주 스트. 클레이르에서, 미국 앨라배마 주 워커에서 약 10년 동안 살았다. 그는 1869년 7월 31일 미국 앨라배마 주 매디슨에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William R Magby родился в 1790 г. в местности хэлифакс, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика, его отцу по имени Vardry Magby II было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Rachel Atkinson, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Dunn 30 мая 1819 г. в местности ст. клеир, Алабама, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он жил в местности ст. клеир, Алабама, Соединенные штаты в 1820 году и в местности валкир, Алабама, Соединенные штаты около 10 лет. Он умер 31 июля 1869 г. в местности мадисон, Алабама, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 79 лет.
